title: Prefix-distilled candidate pairwise scorer

queries: knowledge distillation teacher student neural network
candidate ranking distillation listwise soft labels
shared prefix candidate token scoring distillation

papers.title: Distilling the Knowledge in a Neural Network
papers.claim: A compact student can learn a richer teacher's output distribution through soft-target distillation rather than hard labels alone.
papers.relevance: Stage 3 cheap sample-candidate scorers repeatedly fail to reproduce the candidate-token score surface. Distillation gives the cheap scorer the EXP108-style candidate-token distribution as a training signal without making it the final inference path.

papers.title: Learning to Rank: From Pairwise Approach to Listwise Approach
papers.claim: Ranking can be trained from listwise score distributions rather than only pointwise hard labels.
papers.relevance: The blocker metric is MRR. A teacher distribution over candidates is closer to candidate ranking than another scalar auxiliary or hard-negative weight scan.

evidence.why_now: EXP099 showed candidate-token scoring can make the subset bundle promotable, but the exhaustive hybrid path is not a final Stage4-compatible endpoint. EXP108 preserved item-incoterms near the strict baseline with class-id shared-prefix candidate-token scoring. Later cheap scorer and routing variants repeatedly collapsed around mrr=0.678, showing that hard-label training of the cheap scorer is not recovering the teacher score surface. The next medium-scale action is to distill the EXP108-style prefix teacher into the cheap pairwise scorer during finetune while keeping inference non-autoregressive.

hypothesis: For autocomplete multiclass tasks, the cheap candidate pairwise scorer lacks the candidate-token distributional signal that preserved item-incoterms in EXP108. Training it with a detached class-id shared-prefix teacher distribution should transfer that salt-side score surface into an O(L+C)-style sample-candidate scorer, while avoiding shared-prefix inference, prompt routing variants, residual-scale scans, and old exhaustive O(L*C) rollout.
notes: Draft created after EXP186/187/188 finalized failed and triggered Tier-3 review.
Keep Stage 3 finetune-only; do not use --pretrain.
This is not a shared-prefix rerun as the final scorer: shared-prefix is a detached training teacher only, and eval/test prediction uses the cheap pairwise sample-candidate scorer.
Do not scan distillation weight or temperature manually. Fixed values are used for the initial architecture screen; any value search belongs to later Optuna only if the mechanism earns budget.

Implementation plan: add candidate_pairwise_prefix_distilled, compute student pairwise logits from the prompt representation, compute detached class-id shared-prefix teacher logits during training, add KL soft-target distillation in main.py, and leave eval/test on student logits only.

Stage4 compatibility story: source databases can synthesize categorical-value ranking or value-selection proxy episodes and distill candidate-token/text teacher distributions into the same reusable candidate scorer without true source multiclass benchmark tasks.

Expected failure signature: if item-incoterms remains in the 0.678 collapse band, the cheap pairwise scorer cannot absorb the candidate-token teacher under fixed screening; if throughput is far below EXP108/180 effective items/sec, the teacher path is operationally too expensive.

Implementation added on 2026-05-11 via candidate_pairwise_prefix_distilled and autocomplete_prefix_distill_weight: training computes a detached class-id shared-prefix teacher distribution and adds KL distillation, while eval/test return only pairwise student logits.
Pre-launch validation completed on 2026-05-11: py_compile, git diff --check, JSON validation, and remote item-incoterms shape/throughput sanity all passed.

Remote sanity check completed on 2026-05-11: a 64-step item-incoterms finetune-only run on lab25211 GPU6 completed cleanly with no --pretrain, reached roughly 2.45 train step/s with batch_size=4 (about 9.8 effective train items/sec), and eval/test ran around 14.5 it/s with val_size=1. This is slower than prompt-routing candidates but healthier than the old EXP110 shared-prefix long tail on item-incoterms, which was about 1.28 train step/s and 3.23 test it/s.

Launched on 2026-05-11 with packed batch-equivalent placement: EXP190 user-ltv on GPUs 4,6 with per-rank batch 1 wave 0; EXP189 user-churn on GPU4 with per-rank batch 4 wave 1; EXP191 item-incoterms on GPUs 6,7 with per-rank batch 2 wave 1.
First post-launch status: EXP190 is running, EXP189/191 are queued for wave 1, and orchestrator recommends keep_watching.

Finalized on 2026-05-12 via stage3_orchestrator.py status --sync-logs --recommend-kill. Global verdict: failed. EXP189 user-churn roc_auc=0.6306934031178256, worse than baseline 0.6540209032382359 by -0.023327500120410294 against replay-aware effective delta 0.002552699600237518. EXP190 user-ltv mae=77.16605039620961, neutral because the improvement 2.106522302821247 is inside replay-aware effective delta 6.511244718243262. EXP191 item-incoterms mrr=0.6793995690724206, worse than baseline 0.8147880873466811 by -0.13538851827426046 against replay-aware effective delta 0.013037038915945098.

Throughput was operationally acceptable but not scientifically sufficient: item-incoterms final test ran around 14.6 it/s, and the pre-launch sanity established roughly 9.8 effective train items/sec. The failure is therefore not a practical-throughput failure; the pairwise student did not absorb the prefix teacher's salt-side ranking signal under the fixed screen.

Search-space consequence: stop the small candidate-aware scorer / routing / distillation branch as an active Stage 3 line. EXP180 remains retune-plausible only as a fixed-screen signal, not promotable. Do not manually scan distillation weight, temperature, token budgets, batching, routing, shared-prefix, residual scale, or pairwise scorer variants. Next work should move to larger pipeline blocks: independent GNN representation training and explicit relational invariant preservation during cross-space alignment.

final_results.consequence: Operational throughput was acceptable, but the mechanism failed scientifically. Retire the small candidate-aware scorer/routing/distillation branch as an active Stage 3 line and move to larger pipeline blocks: independent GNN representation training plus explicit relational invariant preservation during cross-space alignment.
